name: Asian Chicken Quesadilla W/Grilled Pineapple Salsa & Hoisin
recipeIngredient: Ingredients Needed for Grilled Pineapple Salsa:
1/2 ripe pineapple - peeled & cored, cut into 1-inch slices
Salt and fresh ground white pepper
recipeInstructions:
Instructions: Prepare the Quesadilla
1. Place the soy sauce, ginger, and garlic in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Remove from heat and let sit for half an hour.
2. Place the chicken breasts in a shallow baking dish and pour the marinade over.
Cover and place in the refrigerator to marinate at least 4 hours or overnight.
3. Season the chicken with salt and pepper to taste. Heat olive oil in a large sauté pan
until it just begins to smoke.
4. Place the chicken in the pan and sauté for 5 minutes or until golden brown on one side.
5. Lower the heat to medium, turn the chicken over and continue cooking until the chicken is almost cooked through, approximately 4 minutes.
6. Let the chicken sit for 1 minute and slice it into 1/2-inch pieces (the chicken will finish cooking in the oven).
7. Preheat the oven to 450°F. Place 2 tortillas on an ungreased baking sheet.
8. Spread half the cheeses, green onion, and sliced chicken on each tortilla.
9. Stack the 2 layers and cover with the remaining tortilla.
10. Bake for 8-12 minutes, or until the tortillas are crisp and the cheese has melted. Cut into quarters and serve hot garnished with the salsa.
Instructions: Prepare the Salsa
1. Heat a grill on high until it just begins to smoke. Grill the pineapple slices 2-3 minutes on each side until lightly browned.
2. Remove and cut into medium dice.
3. Add cilantro, lime, onion, jalapeno, and olive oil.
4. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Let sit at least half an hour.
Instructions: Prepare the Hoisin Vinaigrette
1. Place red onion, garlic, hoisin sauce, and vinegar in a blender and blend until smooth.
2. With the motor running, slowly add sesame and olive oil, drop by drop at first, until emulsified.
3. Season with salt and white pepper to taste.
